entry.title: Re: TLB and PAL UoM
entry.content:
Hi, I just now solved this in my setup.Kindly refer below.

1. Check you have entered tlb profile in tcode /n/sapapo/tl3 for desired locations.

2.Go to /n/sapapo/tlbprf and there you can see your tlb profile, change it to Volume instead pallets.That's it, run TLB error will be resolved.

I hope it helps.

Pratap Bhat

entry.title: Re: How to remove __Metadata from CPI json payload
entry.content:
Hi Akash,

Use below code to remove "__metadata" syntax: 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;
import groovy.json.JsonSlurper;
import groovy.json.JsonBuilder;

def Message processData(Message message)
{
    def body = message.getBody(java.lang.String) as String;
    def jsonParser = new JsonSlurper();
    def data = jsonParser.parseText(body);  
    data.d.results.each{ results ->
    results.remove('__metadata')
    }
    def JsonOutput = new JsonBuilder(data).toPrettyString()
    message.setBody(JsonOutput );
    return message;
}
 
Regards,
Carol Pereira

entry.title: Re: Work Center set up - 3 Machines for combination of Fi...
entry.content:
Hi,

  • Create individual work centers for each testing machine (TM1, TM2, TM3).
  • Create 3 routing groups add operations for each machine that can test to its respective work center  and assign relevant finished goods to it like:
    • Group 1 Operation on Work Center 1 (TM1): FG1, FG3, FG5, FG7, FG9
    • Group 2 Operation on Work Center 2 (TM2): FG2, FG4, FG6, FG8
    • Group 3 Opeartion on Work Center 3 (TM3): FG2, FG3, FG5, FG6, FG9, FG10
  • Finally, create Production versions

Regards

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP开发案例之六：BOM批量读取
entry.content:
紧随这篇博客，今天我们将再次逐步详细地介绍一个利用SAP S/4HANA Cloud三系统来做开发扩展的案例。

 

1. 案例背景

我们有些客户希望查看BOM所有层级的数据，也希望能够批量下载BOM所有层级的数据。我们有标准应用程序“Display BOM level by level”可以查看或下载单个物料的BOM所有层级信息，但是没有应用程序可以查看或下载多个物料的BOM所有层级数据。我们这个案例的开发应用就是来完成这样一个需求。

standard App Display BOM level by level can only view the data for single Material

 

 

通过开发人员可扩展性，我们创建了一个自定义的应用程序，允许用户选择多个物料，可以查看或下载所选物料的所有节点数据。

 

 

2. 后台服务开发

2.1 创建数据定义

创建一个新的数据定义（data definition）

 

 

2.2 创建查询实现类

创建类ZCL_BOM_DISPLAY，在这个类里包含了详细的数据查询逻辑。

 

 

METHOD get_bom_hierarchy.

*get BOM Link and BOM hierarchy based on condition
    SELECT *
    FROM I_BOMComponentWithKeyDate( p_keydate = @RP_keydate ) AS Bom
    JOIN I_MaterialBOMLink AS Bomlink
      ON Bom~BillOfMaterialCategory = Bomlink~BillOfMaterialCategory
     AND Bom~BillOfMaterialVariant = Bomlink~BillOfMaterialVariant
     AND Bom~BillOfMaterial = Bomlink~BillOfMaterial
    WHERE Bomlink~BillOfMaterialCategory = 'M'
    AND Bomlink~BillOfMaterialVariant = '01'
    AND Bomlink~Material IN @RT_material
    AND Bomlink~Plant IN @RT_plant
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_BOMlist).

    IF sy-subrc = 0.
      rv_resp = abap_true.
      CLEAR pt_materialhierarchy.
    ENDIF.
    LOOP AT lt_BOMlist ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_bomlist>).
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING <lfs_bomlist>-bomlink TO ps_response.
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING <lfs_bomlist>-bom TO ps_response.

      "Get product descrption saperatelly since CDS view does not have it.
      SELECT SINGLE ProductName FROM I_producttext
      WHERE Product = @PS_response-material AND Language = @SY-langu INTO @PS_response-materialDesc.

      APPEND ps_response TO pt_response.
      CLEAR ps_response.

      ps_material-sign = 'I'.
      ps_material-option = 'EQ'.
      ps_material-low = <lfs_bomlist>-bom-BillOfMaterialComponent.
      APPEND ps_material TO pt_materialhierarchy.
      CLEAR ps_material.
    ENDLOOP.

  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

2.3 创建服务定义和服务绑定

创建服务定义（Service Definition）

 

创建服务绑定（Service Binding）

 

然后，我们可以预览服务如下：

 

3. 前端应用开发

首先，配置BTP上的destination。

然后，打开SAP Business Application Studio服务

 

从Application Generator里选取List Report Page模板

 

把App部署到SAP S/4hana Public Cloud系统

 

复制Launchpad App Description Item名字到IAM App：

 

通过该新应用，我们不仅可以选择多个物料来查看BOM的节点信息，也可以把所有数据下载到Excel表格，以方便用户线下操作。

 

 

以上是该案例——BOM批量读取自开发应用的步骤介绍。希望通过此案例的介绍能让您了解到三系统开发能实现的常见功能。

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 财务-总账会计常见热点问题汇总FAQ
entry.content:
欢迎来到我们的博客！在这篇文章中，我们将SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition上收集到的产品常见问题做一个汇总并且定期更新这篇文章，以反映SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition上常见问题最新的发展和变化。我们希望通过这样的方式，为您提供一个全面、及时的资源，帮助您更好地了解和利用SAP产品的功能。

本文涉及功能基于SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition 2308版本，后续版本如果有变化请参考本文最新信息。

1总帐会计 – 基本配置

总帐会计。

1.1功能货币  

Q: 如何能够自动的以美元显示报表 ？

A:     请参考链接理解S/4HANA Cloud里的货币，以及功能货币的细节。从1911版本开始支持功能货币的功能呢，财务的核心应用基本都支持功能货币，后续的版本也一直在增加应用对功能货币的支持。 启用功能货币的注意事项，参考“避坑指南”中的相关章节。

1.2银行科目被强制拆分 

Q: 在2008之后凭证分割已经增强了对科目拆分的逻辑，所以在过账时会根据对方科目上的利润中心来拆分出多条行项目。而且只支持对银行科目和公司代码清账科目这两类资产负债表科目，不支持对缺省利润中心目的支持。 

A:     如果不想拆分银行科目或者公司代码清账科目，请参照SSCUI 102529来对科目分配缺省利润中心。 

1.3凭证分割的设置 

Q: 是否可以取消凭证分割的功能？

A:     SAP不建议客户在生产系统中开启或关闭凭证分割功能，因为会导致更改前后的文档会有不一致。Cloud系统的配置是标准化的，凭证分割功能默认是激活的。  

1.4重分类应收、应付科目的SSCUI在什么地方 

Q: 如题

A:     SSCUI 100297,进入如下路径：财务会计-> 总账会计-> 传输并排序应收账款和应付账款 

1.5如何找到仓库收发货货物移动的会计科目配置点 

Q: 如题

A:  有两个方法， 第一个方法，找到收发货货物移动产生的财务凭证，更改布局，找到字段‘Trs‘，即‘交易码’ 。然后在SSCUI 100297里找到配置点：范围‘货源确定和采购’交易组‘RMK’交易吗‘UMB’科目表‘YCOA’ 。在科目配置进行科目分配。第二个方法，找到过账的物料的会计科目， 通过app管理总账科目主数据的使用位置。

1.6税码 

Q: 在Cloud上如何维护税码？

A:       如下

 

关于如何在S/4HANA Cloud创建税码，请参阅注释3009061 - How to create tax code in S/4Hana Cloud 

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3009061 

 

关于如何在S/4HANA Cloud删除税码，请参阅注释3092673 - Delete tax code in S4HC 

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3092673 

 

关于如何在S/4HANA Cloud创建基于时间的税码（TDT），请参阅注释2936931 - TDT S/4Hana Cloud 

https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2936931 

 

 

1.7一次性把所有的科目从现有公司扩充新公司代码的方法 

Q: 如题

A:       使用app 管理总账科目主数据，选择公司代码视图下，使用功能‘复制到公司代码’, 但是最多一次选择500个科目。如果提示权限不够 ，得有标准角色: SAP_BR_BPC_EXPER的权限 

 

1.8会计科目表是否允许新增？ 

Q: 如题

A:       基于云产品的理念，快速实施以及标准化流程，S/4HANA Cloud上有预交付的标准运营会计科目表，命名为YCOA供全球用户使用，而且是不能删除的.它会默认分配给所有公司代码,而且不允许更改这个分配。 

 截止到2108版本，已经提供配置步骤/应用让客户可以把标准的运营/组/备选科目表转换成客户的运营/组/备选科目表，包括：科目长度可以自己定义（通过配置更改），批量重新定义科目描述，批量重新定义科目编码，客户备选科目表 

编码可以是字母数字混合编码，可以选择控制不使用的部分科目不传输到P系统，批量上传和下载科目到MS excel进行批量调整， 

 关于运营科目表/组科目表/备选科目表的详细信息，可以参考如下链接来进一步理解 

https://help.sap.com/viewer/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/LATEST/zh-CN/9f53c2531bb9b44ce10000000a174cb4.html 

 

1.9快速批量创建科目到生产系统？ 

Q: 某些在Q系统创建的测试科目不需要传输或者同步到生产系统，该如何操作？

A:       使用app总账科目同步, 在Q和P系统执行一下，就OK了 

 

1.10可以不传输或者同步某些科目到生产系统吗？ 

Q: 如题

A:       可以在app管理总账科目主数据里，勾选不使用的总账科目 

 

1.11设置总帐科目的未清项管理 

Q: 如题

A:       可以使用app 计划会计核算数据更正来进行处理。成功之后，该科目的历史业务凭证也会变成未清项凭证，可以进行手工或者自动清账处理。 

参考帮助文档链接 

参考Fiori链接 

 

 

2总账会计- 业务操作

如题。

2.1会计年度变式和自动开关账期

Q: 如题。如何设置和取舍？

A:       默认交付的会计年度变式是K4，其中包含4个特殊期间,便于记录调整业务的数据，可以放到13-16期间；app管理过账期间。但是如果使用含有特殊期间的会计年度变式，就无法使用app管理过账期间里的后台作业实现自动开关账期的功能了。 

详情，请参见注释2896867 - Manage Posting Periods app (F2293): Failed to schedule recurrence because the item contains special periods 

 

 

2.2科目余额可否以交易货币显示？

Q: 如题。因为客户有外币业务，现在显示科目余额的APP都是人民币，但是财务人员要已交易货币（例如港币，美元）显示科目余额的APP，方便查看。是否有这样的APP？ 

A:       可以用app 试算平衡表。在导航面板里的列 – 度量 里 选择 交易货币的四个度量 ，就可以实现了。 

 

2.3开具供应商发票时，选择的采购订单行项目不能超过999行项目

Q: 我们一个月同一个供应商涉及的采购订单行项目涉及几千行，开供应商发票，过账无法超过999行财务凭证行项目，如要拆成好多张，非常麻烦

A:       参考这个注释来操作吧 https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2845836 另外参照博文https://blogs.sap.com/?p=1364264来进一步了解操作 

2.4 App管理过账期间的操作日志

Q: 在一个用户操作了过账期间之后，想查看操作的日期、时间和用户信息 

A:       截止到最新版本，没有这个功能。参考注释3028569 - There are no change logs for "Manage Posting Periods" 

同时，也请投票到https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/242132以便把您的需求传递到开发部门。 

2.5 app过账含清账被弃用之后应收、应付、预付款及往来账户调整和清账的业务如何处理？ 

Q: 从2111版本开始过账含清账会被删除，用户无法使用它来处理业务。 

A:       请参照如下博文进行操作 

1 

从S/4 HANA Cloud ES 2102总账科目手工清账使用的应用变化谈起来 

https://blogs.sap.com/?p=1272181 

2 

S/4 HANA Cloud 2102开始弃用app过账（含清账）之后对应收和应付的调整如何操作？ 

https://blogs.sap.com/?p=1282259 

3 

S/4 HANA Cloud 2102 开始弃用app过账（含清账）之后对预付款业务如何操作？ 

https://blogs.sap.com/?p=1299107 

4 

浅谈S/4HANA Cloud里如何处理特殊总账标识业务-大结局 

https://blogs.sap.com/?p=1307755 

5 

浅谈如何在S/4HANA Cloud里对未清项使用和实现收付款的剩余金额功能 

https://blogs.sap.com/?p=1289105 

 

2.6 客户第一次经历独自年结，希望可以分享各模块余额结转和账期结转的最佳实践案例 FICO、MM、SD、PP各模块是否都有年结相关流程。 

Q: 如题。 

A: 请参见链接 

 

2.7 同时对多个公司开启关闭过账期间 

Q: 如题。 

A:       需求的客户，可以投票到https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/255206/?section=sectionDetails 

 

2.8 集成汇率到SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

Q:      如题。 

A:       需求的客户，可以投票到https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/255206/?section=sectionDetails 

 

客户的需求汇总在CIP Public API to integrate Exchange Rate in S4H Public Cloud 

需要如下两个SI能实现，不会提供单独的API 

1S4 (Market Rates Management - Automatically via SAP Cloud Platform),  

https://rapid.sap.com/bp/#/browse/scopeitems/1S4 

1XN (Market Rates Management - Manually via Upload) 

https://rapid.sap.com/bp/#/browse/scopeitems/1XN 

 

3总帐-报表

3.1如何按照利润中心、业务范围、段出示报表？ 

Q:      如题。 

A:       预交付的系统的默认配置没有勾选，利润中心必输，这意味着凭证里所有的行项目不一定都会有利润中心的值，因此利润中心的报表会缺失数据，导致信息不完整。 

请参考2868475 - SSCUI: Define Document Splitting Characteristics for General Ledger。 

只有提交事件，要求SAP提供专家配置可以用来更改此设置，可以通过CSM请求。 

需要注意的是， 在项目实施阶段，激活此配置，并进行业务测试，可以最大化的实现需求； 

如果上线后才激活此配置，需要进行大量的业务测试，非常耗费人力。 

请一定慎重考虑。 

 

3.2现金流量表

Q:      如题。 

A:       现金流量表有新旧两个方案，都提供直接法和间接法两种方法，都有配置点； 

旧的现金流量表方案已经在2019.12.31终止后续功能增强，老方案在J58中; 

新的方案基于高级合规报表 

新方案在1J2里，并且同时需要J78，须额外授权费。 

参见如下链接了解， 

https://help.sap.com/viewer/147436affce24e81892eee2076a78116/2105.500/zh-CN/361bb7e78a1e4077840fa406417eb0dc.html 

 

4总帐-权限

4.1是否可以在这个app管理过账期间上实现功能上的控制

Q:      是否可以在这个app（管理过账期间）上实现功能上的控制，比如A用户只能进行操作1，不能进行操作2。 CO期间这个操作会关系到所有的分公司，希望只有财务经理可以进行操作。 

A:       请通过限制类型 - 公司代码/分类账/业务交易类型 来实现, 通过授权或者不授权业务交易类型来给予该用户是否可以对CO期间的访问和操作。同时可以参照博文进一步了解现在Cloud上能实现的权限控制的实例。 

4.2版本升级之后发现SSCUI无权限进入，如何处理？

Q:      如题 

A:       参照注释来操作 3071670 - Unable to access or configure SSCUI in Configure your Solution https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3071670

entry.title: IFlow getting into error status even with End message event in exception sub process in SAP CPI
entry.content:
Hi,

In one of our sync interface, we have an exception sub process with error start event and end message event. We added it deliberately because we want to send custom response message to sender instead of Internal server error 500.

This was working fine , but recently (don't know whether its an update or change by SAP) with end message also we are getting Internal server error 500 and getting to failed status in monitoring.

Is there any other way of returning custom response to sender ?

entry.title: Re: Announcement: New release of content and central entry point for SAP BTP Solution Diagrams
entry.content:
@MioYasuatke You can make use of the AIO config here:

https://sap.github.io/btp-solution-diagrams/docs/solution_diagr_intro/intro/#add-sap-colors-btp-icons-logos-etc-to-drawio-configuration-at-once

You can then add globally the libraries w/o adding anytime the lib's again (only in case of changes/new icons)

Did this help?

 

thx and best,

Fabian

entry.title: Re: How to handle if-match header via BTP destination?
entry.content:
If I understood correctly, when you retrieve the record you want to update, you store the ETAG in a page variable. You will have to create a REST data resource with destination, and set up the PATTCH call "manually" and there you can send a header that is bound to that page variable.

I don't know if there is some automated way to do this, or whether SAP Build Apps handles the ETAG for you.

I think when SAP Build Apps uses actions for data access some of this will be simpler.

entry.title: How to bind xsuaa details in deployment yaml to authenticate api in SAP BTP kyma runtime
entry.content:
"Hello community,

I am currently working with SAP BTP Kyma Runtime and aiming to bind XSUAA details in the deployment YAML file to enable API authentication from the Kyma runtime. Any blogs or references on this topic would be highly appreciated.

 

apiVersion: services.cloud.sap.com/v1alpha1
kind: ServiceInstance
metadata:
  name: xsuaa-apiservice
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: xsuaa
spec:
  serviceOfferingName: xsuaa
  servicePlanName: application
  externalName: xsuaa-apiservice
  parameters:
    xsappname: myxsuaa-apiservice
    tenant-mode: dedicated
    oauth2-configuration:
      redirect-uris:
        - "https://api.kyma.ondemand.com/login/callback"
    scopes:
      - name: "$XSAPPNAME.admin"
        description: Admin
    role-templates:
      - name: admin
        description: Admin
        scope-references:
          - "$XSAPPNAME.admin"
    role-collections:
      - name: Approuterkymaxsuaaservice
        description: kyma approuter role
        role-template-references:
          - "$XSAPPNAME.admin"

---

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: xsuaa-apiservice
  labels:
    app: xsuaa
    
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: xsuaa
      
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: xsuaa
        
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: xsuaa-apiservice
          image: shobhittyagi
          imagePullPolicy: Always
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "128Mi"
            requests:
              cpu: "250m"
              memory: "128Mi"
          ports:
            - containerPort: 8086
          volumeMounts:
            - name: xsuaa-apiservice
              mountPath: "/etc/secrets/sapcp/xsuaa/xsuaa-apiservice"
              readOnly: true
          env:
            - name: xsuaa-apiservice
              value: '{"xsuaa":[{"credentials":{
      volumes:
        - name: xsuaa-apiservice
          secret:
            secretName: apiservicexsuaa-binding

---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: xsuaa-apiservice1
  labels:
    app: xsuaa
    service: xsuaa
spec:
  ports:
    - port: 8086
      name: http
  selector:
    app: xsuaa

entry.title: About Workarea Folder Structure in SAP Enable Now for Simulations & In-App Content
entry.content:
Please help with your inputs on the Workarea folder structure as requested below:

Scenario 01:

In the SAP Enable Now, we created a new regular Workarea. The Workarea shows the Content folder automatically created under Unsorted, as shown in the picture below.

Now, under the Contents folder, to store simulations, I will create the subfolders, such as Creation, Maintenance, and Publish as explained in the Master Author course and in this Community blog. 

My Question: Should the Content folder remain under Unsorted, or should I manually move it to some other location in the Workarea, for example, Root Group? What is the best practice for the same?

Scenario 02:

In the second scenario, we created an Extended Workarea based on a standard Workarea. The extended workarea does not show the Content folder created automatically under Unsorted, as shown in the picture below:

Question 01: 

Here, I want to create subfolders to store simulations as well In-App guided tutorials. So, should I manually create the Content folder first under Unsorted, and then create the subfolders, such as "Creation, Maintenance, and Publish"?

Finally, should my subfolders be under Unsorted only, or need to be moved to some other location in the Extended workarea? What's the best practice here?

Question 02: 

For the In-App content as well, should I use the similar folder structure such as "Creation, Maintenance, and Publish" like simulations? OR, we generally do not duplicate the In-App Guided Tour shells under Creation, Maintenance, and Publish folders separately. What is the best practice here?

Thanking you in advance for your inputs.

entry.title: Re: Clock In Clock Out in SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking – Overview and How-To Guide
entry.content:
Hi Uumakant,

The time type Id is an optional field in the request body for the terminal API. Please refer to the documentation - REST API for Sending Time Events from Terminal to Clock In Clock Out | SAP Help Portal

The P10 time type event Id can be used for all the time types and when there is a pair created, they will be assigned with the associated time type sent from the terminal.

Regards

Neetha

entry.title: Re: Hello dears i need this scope item to be localized at...
entry.content:
Hi,

Event-Based Production Cost Posting (scope item 3F0) available for all countries.

Event-Based Production Accounting (3F0) 

Event-Based (3F0) versus Period-Based (BEI) 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Hello dears i need this scope item to be localized at...
entry.content: 3F0 has already been the default scope item for production cost posting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition since year 2021 for the newly implemented customers. Only with rare valid business reasons would SAP approve the request to activate the optional scope item BEI for newly implemented customers. Now SAP is announcing the deprecation of BEI, which means the customers went live before year 2021 and the customers went live after year 2021 but also requested to use BEI are supposed to transition from BEI to 3F0 before BEI is completely retired.

entry.title: How to replace the output "ORDER_CONFIRMATION_CHANGE" with "ORDER_CONFIRMATION"?
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

Our business needs to modify sales orders before sending to customers. We need to send the initial document instead of the changed document.

We'd like to know how to replace the output "ORDER_CONFIRMATION_CHANGE" with "ORDER_CONFIRMATION".

The goal is to use the output "ORDER_CONFIRMATION" for any changes done in the sales order.

Thanks and regards.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to replace the output "ORDER_CONFIRMATION_CHANGE"...
entry.content:
You can consider the below two options:

Option 1: Configure "Output Relevance" in the "Output Parameter Determination" app, to restrict the output to happen only after all the conditions are met (that is, after your required changes are done from your side).

Option 2: Reach out to your local consultant/developer to change the design of the form template "ORDER_CONFIRMATION_CHANGE" in Adobe LiveCycle Designer, e.g. to remove any scripts that may show that this is a "changed" version of sales order.

Please be noticed that, according to KBA 2984817 - Form design in Adobe LiveCycle Designer, it is the customer who is responsible for implementing custom forms using Adobe Livecycle Designer. In other words, form customization is not in-scope of a standard SAP implementation. So you're suggested to reach out to your local consultant/developer for this issue.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP开发案例之七：库龄报表
entry.content:
紧随这篇博客，今天我们将再次逐步详细地介绍一个利用SAP S/4HANA Cloud三系统来做开发扩展的案例。

 

1. 案例背景

库龄报表是大多数客户都会询问和想要的一张报表。它以逆时间顺序概述了一段时间内从选定的关键日期开始的可用物料库存的分布情况。但是，具体的需求可能因客户而异。例如，一些客户可能希望在计算物料库龄信息时只考虑某些移动类型。通过利用开发人员可扩展性，我们开发了一个定制的库龄报表，并嵌入到SAP S/4hana Cloud系统中。此外，客户可以完全灵活地定义哪些移动类型将包括在库龄报告的计算中。

 

同时，我们配置了一个Custom Business Configuration项目，我们可以把希望被统计在库龄报表里的移动类型维护在这个表格内，如下图。

 

2. 后台服务开发

2.1 创建数据定义

创建一个新的数据定义（Data Definition）：Z_MATERIAL_STOCK_AGE

 

2.2 创建查询实现类

创建类ZCL_MATERIAL_STOCK_AGE，在这个类里包含了基于CDS视图的数据查询逻辑。

 

CLASS zcl_material_stock_age DEFINITION
  PUBLIC
  FINAL
  CREATE PUBLIC .

  PUBLIC SECTION.

    INTERFACES if_rap_query_provider .
  PROTECTED SECTION.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
ENDCLASS.



CLASS ZCL_MATERIAL_STOCK_AGE IMPLEMENTATION.


  METHOD if_rap_query_provider~select.
    IF io_request->is_data_requested( ).

      TYPES BEGIN OF ty_value.
      TYPES: quantity TYPE i_stockquantitycurrentvalue_2-matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit,
             value    TYPE i_stockquantitycurrentvalue_2-stockvalueindisplaycurrency.
      TYPES END OF ty_value.

      TYPES:
        BEGIN OF ty_range_option,
          sign   TYPE c LENGTH 1,
          option TYPE c LENGTH 2,
          low    TYPE string,
          high   TYPE string,
        END OF ty_range_option.


      DATA: lt_response        TYPE TABLE OF z_material_stock_age,
            ls_response        LIKE LINE OF lt_response,
            lt_responseout     LIKE lt_response,
            ls_responseout     LIKE LINE OF lt_responseout,
            lt_response_period TYPE TABLE OF z_material_stock_age,
            ls_response_period LIKE LINE OF lt_response_period,
            lt_value           TYPE TABLE OF ty_value,
            ls_value           TYPE ty_value,
            lv_price           TYPE p DECIMALS 2,
            lt_configr         TYPE TABLE OF ty_range_option,
            ls_configr         LIKE LINE OF lt_configr.


      DATA(lv_top)           = io_request->get_paging( )->get_page_size( ).
      DATA(lv_skip)          = io_request->get_paging( )->get_offset( ).
      DATA(lv_max_rows) = COND #( WHEN lv_top = if_rap_query_paging=>page_size_unlimited THEN 0
                                  ELSE lv_top ).

      DATA(lt_clause)        = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_ranges( ).
      DATA(lt_parameter)     = io_request->get_parameters( ).
      DATA(lt_fields)        = io_request->get_requested_elements( ).
      DATA(lt_sort)          = io_request->get_sort_elements( ).

      TRY.
          DATA(lt_filter_cond) = io_request->get_filter( )->get_as_ranges( ).
        CATCH cx_rap_query_filter_no_range INTO DATA(lx_no_sel_option).
      ENDTRY.

****************************Data selection and business logics goes here*********************************
      LOOP AT lt_parameter ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<fs_p>).
        CASE <fs_p>-parameter_name.
          WHEN 'P_KEYDATE'.   DATA(p_keydate) = <fs_p>-value.
        ENDCASE.
      ENDLOOP.

      LOOP AT lt_filter_cond INTO DATA(ls_filter_cond).
        IF ls_filter_cond-name = 'STROAGELOCATION'.
          DATA(lt_storagelocation) = ls_filter_cond-range[].
        ELSEIF  ls_filter_cond-name = 'MATERIAL'.
          DATA(lt_material) = ls_filter_cond-range[].
        ELSEIF  ls_filter_cond-name = 'PLANT'.
          DATA(lt_plant) = ls_filter_cond-range[].
        ELSEIF  ls_filter_cond-name = 'COMPANYCODE'.
          DATA(lt_bukrs) = ls_filter_cond-range[].
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

*get configuration data
      SELECT * FROM zmovementtype WHERE bukrs IN @LT_bukrs
                                  AND inuse = @abap_true
                                  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_config).

      LOOP AT lt_config INTO DATA(ls_config).
        ls_configr-sign = 'I'.
        ls_configr-option = 'EQ'.
        ls_configr-low = ls_config-movementtype.
        APPEND ls_configr TO lt_configr.
        CLEAR ls_configr.
      ENDLOOP.

*get inventory information based on date
      SELECT *
      FROM i_materialstock_2
      WHERE storagelocation IN @LT_storagelocation
      AND material IN @LT_material
      AND matldoclatestpostgdate <= @p_keydate
      AND plant IN @LT_plant
      AND companycode IN  @LT_bukrs
      INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_stock).

      LOOP AT lt_stock INTO DATA(ls_stock).
        ls_response-companycode = ls_stock-companycode.
        ls_response-plant = ls_stock-plant.
        ls_response-material = ls_stock-material.
        ls_response-stroagelocation = ls_stock-storagelocation.
        ls_response-currentstock = ls_stock-matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit.
        COLLECT ls_response INTO lt_response.
      ENDLOOP.

*period start date calculation
      DATA lv_30 TYPE d.
      DATA lv_60 TYPE d.
      DATA cd TYPE d.
      cd = p_keydate.
      lv_30 = cd - 30.
      lv_60 = cd - 60.

*get material movement history
      SELECT *
      FROM i_materialdocumentitem_2
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN @LT_response
      WHERE storagelocation = @LT_response-stroagelocation
      AND postingdate <= @p_keydate
      AND plant IN @LT_plant
      AND companycode IN @LT_bukrs
      AND material = @LT_response-material
      AND goodsmovementtype in @LT_configr
      AND goodsmovementiscancelled IS INITIAL
      INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_movement).

      DATA(lt_movement_reverse) = lt_movement[].

      LOOP AT lt_movement INTO DATA(ls_movement).
        READ TABLE lt_movement_reverse WITH KEY  reversedmaterialdocumentyear = ls_movement-materialdocumentyear
                                                 reversedmaterialdocument = ls_movement-materialdocument
                                       TRANSPORTING NO FIELDS.
        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          DELETE lt_movement FROM ls_movement.
        ENDIF.
      ENDLOOP.

      LOOP AT lt_movement INTO ls_movement WHERE reversedmaterialdocument IS INITIAL.
        ls_response_period-companycode = ls_movement-companycode.
        ls_response_period-plant = ls_movement-plant.
        ls_response_period-material = ls_movement-material.
        ls_response_period-stroagelocation = ls_movement-storagelocation.
        ls_response_period-meins = ls_movement-materialbaseunit.
        ls_response_period-waers = ls_movement-companycodecurrency.

        IF ls_movement-postingdate > lv_30 AND ls_movement-postingdate <= cd.
          ls_response_period-period1 = ls_movement-quantityinbaseunit.
        ELSEIF ls_movement-postingdate > lv_60 AND ls_movement-postingdate <= lv_30.
          ls_response_period-period2 = ls_movement-quantityinbaseunit.
        ELSEIF ls_movement-postingdate <= lv_60.
          ls_response_period-period3 = ls_movement-quantityinbaseunit.
        ENDIF.

        COLLECT ls_response_period INTO lt_response_period.
        CLEAR ls_response_period.
      ENDLOOP.

      LOOP AT lt_response INTO ls_response.
        READ TABLE lt_response_period WITH KEY companycode = ls_response-companycode
                                               plant = ls_response-plant
                                               material = ls_response-material
                                               stroagelocation = ls_response-stroagelocation
                                      INTO ls_response_period.

*        ls_response-meins = ls_response_period-meins.
*        ls_response-waers = ls_response_period-waers.

        IF sy-subrc = 0.
* period 1 calculation with 30 days.
          IF ls_response-currentstock < ls_response_period-period1 AND ls_response_period-period1 > 0.
            ls_response-period1 = ls_response-currentstock.
          ELSE.
            ls_response-period1 = ls_response_period-period1.
          ENDIF.
* period 2 calculation between 30 days to 60 days.
          IF ( ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 )  < 0.
            ls_response-period2 = 0.
          ELSEIF ( ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 ) < ls_response_period-period2 AND ls_response_period-period2 > 0.
            ls_response-period2 = ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1.
          ELSEIF ( ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 ) >= ls_response_period-period2.
            ls_response-period2 = ls_response_period-period2.
          ENDIF.
* period 3 calculation more than 60 days
          IF ( ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 - ls_response_period-period2 )  < 0.
            ls_response-period3 = 0.
          ELSEIF ( ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 - ls_response_period-period2 ) < ls_response_period-period3 AND ls_response_period-period3 > 0.
            ls_response-period3 = ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 - ls_response_period-period2.
          ELSEIF ( ls_response-currentstock - ls_response_period-period1 - ls_response_period-period2 ) >= ls_response_period-period3.
            ls_response-period3 = ls_response_period-period3.
          ENDIF.

        ELSEIF sy-subrc > 0 AND ls_response-currentstock > 0.
          ls_response-period3 = ls_response-currentstock.
        ENDIF.

* getting price information
        SELECT SUM( matlwrhsstkqtyinmatlbaseunit ) AS quantity,
               SUM( stockvalueincccrcy ) AS value
               FROM i_stockquantitycurrentvalue_2( p_displaycurrency = 'CNY' )
               WHERE product = @LS_response-material
               AND plant = @LS_response-plant
               AND storagelocation = @LS_response-stroagelocation
               AND valuationareatype = 1
               GROUP BY product,plant,storagelocation
               INTO TABLE @LT_value.

        IF sy-subrc = 0.
          CLEAR: ls_value,
                 lv_price.
          READ TABLE lt_value INTO ls_value INDEX 1.
          IF ls_value-quantity NE 0.
            lv_price = ls_value-value / ls_value-quantity.
          ENDIF.
        ENDIF.

* calculate value information
        ls_response-currentvalue = ls_response-currentstock * lv_price.
        ls_response-period1_v = ls_response-period1 * lv_price.
        ls_response-period2_v = ls_response-period2 * lv_price.
        ls_response-period3_v = ls_response-period3 * lv_price.

        MODIFY lt_response FROM ls_response.
        CLEAR ls_response.
      ENDLOOP.

*paging way to return huge amount of data
      SORT lt_response BY stroagelocation material.
      lv_max_rows = lv_skip + lv_top.
      IF lv_skip > 0.
        lv_skip = lv_skip + 1.
      ENDIF.

      CLEAR lt_responseout.
      LOOP AT lt_response ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_out_line_item>) FROM lv_skip TO lv_max_rows.
        ls_responseout = <lfs_out_line_item>.
        APPEND ls_responseout TO lt_responseout.
      ENDLOOP.

      io_response->set_total_number_of_records( lines( lt_response ) ).
      io_response->set_data( lt_responseout ).

    ENDIF.
  ENDMETHOD.
ENDCLASS.

 

 

2.3 创建服务定义和服务绑定

创建服务定义（Service Definition）

 

创建服务绑定（Service Binding）

我们可以看到服务预览如下：

 

3. 前台应用开发

首先，在BTP上配置destination。

然后，打开SAP Business Application Studio服务。

 

从Application Generator里选取SAP Fiori Worklist Application模板。

 

把应用部署到SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud系统。

 

以上是该案例——库龄报表自开发应用的步骤介绍。希望通过此案例的介绍能让您了解到三系统开发能实现的常见功能。

  • Topic pages: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics where information about SAP products from SAP teams is provided
  • Blog posts: https://community.sap.com/t5/all-sap-community-blogs/ct-p/all-blogs to read experience and knowledge from other community members or here, you can share your own expertise

Don't forget to subscribe to the specific topics your are interested in.

entry.title: Business Process Management Games: Learning from Business Process Optimization Research
entry.content:
Introduction 

A key challenge in Business Process Management (BPM) is making business process improvement tangible and actionable. The most important aspects of this challenge are the following: 

  1. Making BPM accessible, fostering a BPM culture. 
  1. Making BPM actionable, facilitating process improvements and performance gains. 

While the former aspect is a prerequisite for the latter, the two are necessarily intertwined. Without an organizational BPM culture that ensures buy-in and BPM literacy among all relevant stakeholders, meaningful and effective change cannot be achieved, even with a theoretically mature toolbox at hand. Still, in our data-driven world, advanced intelligence approaches are required to optimize processes in complex organizations in a sustainable manner, under the responsible considerations of efforts and risks. This interconnection also manifests itself in classical symptoms of poor BPM, such as the traditional ivory tower center of process excellence, modeling “wallpapers” of process models that are detached from operational reality or the more contemporary problem of expensive process mining projects that lead to fancy dashboards but not to substantial change. 

To address this challenge, we draw upon the research results and experience of Professor Remco Dijkman, a seasoned business process optimization research leader at Eindhoven University of Technology. Specifically, in internal pilots of SAP Signavio BPM Academy, we use the BPM Game developed by Remco and his group to bring colleagues who are new to BPM (think of software engineers coming from a different domain, such as consumer software) up-to-speed regarding the objectives and challenges of modern BPM. Beyond that, we are glad to expand our horizon and learn more about business process optimization research that – in contrast to the often-emphasized work utilizing discrete approaches such as Petri nets – draws from a wide variety of quantitative methods in well-established research domains such as operations research to address the complex challenges of real-world BPM in an interconnected world. Below, we provide a glimpse into both aspects of the fruitful exchange. 

Prof. Remco Dijkman with SAP experts and senior managers in front of the Berlin SAP Office.

The BPM Game 

The BPM game [1] is a gamified business process simulator that centers around improving a business process, initially developed to provide BPM students with a more tangible, hands-on learning experience. In contrast to traditional business process simulators, the BPM game has the following key differentiating characteristics: 

  • It is easier to use, in that it focuses on more than process models and event logs, instead covering the broader business context in a way that non-academics would intuitively expect. 
  • It focuses on a realistic business process optimization scenario and tells a broader business story, beyond mere technicalities of process model and event log. 
  • The simulator that underlies the game is prone to diverge from the “model-based” behaviors that have been explicitly specified. For example, if the process model specifies behavior that does not correspond to what the customer expects or according to which employees cannot do their work, the virtual employees in the simulator will try to “fix” the process by running extra tasks. With this, the game provides a more realistic simulation of the actual business environment (relative to traditional simulators), in which work is not always executed in the exact way that a process model or resource schedule specifies. 

So far, SAP Signavio has run the game with hundreds of colleagues, receiving tremendously positive feedback from users. We expect that the underlying ideas can be useful for customers (i.e., BPM practitioners) as well, in that the game can help upskill employees in terms of BPM knowledge in a realistic yet entertaining manner, thus fostering a BPM culture. 

Opening the Technical Toolbox for Next-Generation BPM

Looking further into the future, we are excited about several lines of research that Remco’s group is advancing, applying tools and methods from AI, computer science, and operation research in a novel and innovative way to business process management. 

Stochastic programming for process outcome prediction and optimization [2]. Utilizing probabilistic approaches (classical, as well as machine learning-based) when planning and optimizing organizational operations helps generate more realistic assessments, better informed decisions, and ultimately reduced operational costs.  

Counterfactual analysis for identifying process improvements [3]. Utilizing both expert knowledge and data, process patterns that affect process performance and whose enforcement (or avoidance) can thus yield to better business results can be identified, bridging the gap from insights about a process to actions that affect meaningful change. 

Policy learning for business decisions and resource assignments. Using reinforcement learning approaches, future business process simulators and execution systems may learn the best or most realistic resource assignment or decision policies. 

We are looking forward to diving deeper into these topics and to get inspired for next-generation product capabilities. 

The Road Ahead 

Drawing from the research and experience of academic experts such as Remco is crucial to stay ahead of the curve in the fast-moving field of business process management & intelligence software. We are looking forward to continuing the exchange with Remco and his group, not only to ensure that all SAP Signavio colleagues are up-to-speed on the basics of BPM, but also to facilitate innovation with respect to the capabilities we provide to our customers.

References

[1] Dijkman, R., & Peters, S. The Business Process Management Game. Business Process Management Demos. 2019.

[2] V. Gumuskaya, W. van Jaarsveld, R. Dijkman, P. Grefen, A. Veenstra. Integrating stochastic programs and decision trees in capacitated barge planning with uncertain container arrivals. Transportation Research. 2021. 

[3] Vazifehdoostirani, M., Genga, L., Lu, X., Verhoeven, R., van Laarhoven, H., & Dijkman, R. M. Interactive Multi-interest Process Pattern Discovery. Business Process Management. 2023.

entry.title: Unleashing the Power of Artificial Intelligence in SAP Customer Support
entry.content:
Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) have revolutionized the way businesses operate, opening doors to unprecedented opportunities for innovation and efficiency. Within the realm of customer support at SAP, these cutting-edge technologies play a crucial role in enhancing the customer experience and streamlining support processes.

Customer support is the backbone of any successful business, and at SAP, we understand the importance of delivering exceptional service to our customers. With the help of AI and LLMs, we are transforming our support offerings to provide unparalleled support capabilities and elevate the customer experience to new heights.

One of the key areas where AI and LLMs are making a significant impact is in accelerating response times and resolving customer queries with greater accuracy. Through advanced natural language processing capabilities, AI-powered chatbots can understand and respond to customer inquiries in real-time, providing immediate assistance and reducing the burden on human support agents.

Moreover, AI-driven analytics tools enable us to analyze vast amounts of support data to identify trends, patterns, and recurring issues, allowing us to proactively address customer needs and prevent future problems from occurring. This predictive, bi-directional support approach ensures that customers receive the support they need before they even realize they need it, leading to increased satisfaction and loyalty.

Additionally, AI-powered recommendation engines can suggest relevant solutions and resources to customers based on their specific needs and preferences, further enhancing the support experience and empowering customers to find solutions independently.

It's important to note that SAP's customer support team acts as a trailblazer in leveraging AI and LLMs to improve processes and drive efficiency. By harnessing the full potential of these technologies internally, we not only enhance our own support capabilities but also serve as a blueprint for other SAP customers looking to leverage AI in their own businesses.

As we look ahead to SAP Sapphire 2024, taking place from June 3 to June 5 in Orlando and from June 11 to June 13 in Barcelona, we invite you to join us for sessions that delve deeper into the transformative power of AI in customer support:

Translating Generative AI and LLMs into Business Benefits (Orlando: SER207)
Explore how generative AI and large language models (LLMs) are reshaping customer support offerings, enhancing internal efficiencies, and accelerating the customer experience. Learn about the design, implementation, and integration of LLMs to elevate your support capabilities to new heights.

Reinventing Support with AI (Orlando: SER202 / Barcelona: SER221)
Dive into how intelligent automation and AI are transforming support offerings to deliver unparalleled customer experiences. Gain insights into predictive, bi-directional support and explore our journey towards delivering outstanding value to customers.

Release the Value of AI in Your Business Transformation (Orlando: SER203 / Barcelona: SER222)
Witness the transformative power of AI integration into business processes, demonstrated by our customer support team. Explore the tangible value AI brings to your digital transformation journey through SAP Signavio solutions.

Lookout for the sessions at SAP Sapphire 2024 to discover how AI is reshaping the future of customer support and learn how you can harness its potential to drive success in your own business. Ensure to book some time with our experts within the Services and Support Center at both SAP Sapphire events, Orlando and Barcelona 2024: Register now.

#SAP #ArtificialIntelligence #CustomerSupport #SAPSapphire2024 #Innovation #DigitalTransformation #LargeLanguageModels ##LLMs

All sections contain subsections with fragments inside.

Attachments are loaded with

Any help would be appreciated.
Regards,
Deniz Ozturk

entry.title: Need CDS Views for Material Document(Good receipt and Supplier invoice)
entry.content:
We need to join the Material document with the supplier invoice data source for Client requirement purposes. We tried but we were unable to find any data source that links the Material Document and Supplier invoice.

Please Check and help us to solve the issues.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Need CDS Views for Material Document(Good receipt and...
entry.content:
To meet your requirement you need to create a custom CDS view on top of I_SuplrInvcItemPurOrdRefAPI01 and I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2.

From I_SuplrInvcItemPurOrdRefAPI01 you can read reference to material document is stored in field REFERENCEDOCUMENT and REFERENCEDOCUMENTFISCALYEAR. Then you can join its view against I_MATERIALDOCUMENTITEM_2 (using REFERENCEDOCUMENT + REFERENCEDOCUMENTFISCALYEAR from the invoice view against fields MATERIALDOCUMENT and MATERIALDOCUMENTYEAR of the mentioned material document view).

entry.title: Re: Master data retransfer from IBP to SAP ECC or S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi Gaurav,

thanks for your reply. In general I am clear about how to achieve it. I was hoping for a more concrete approach. Maybe I can frame my question a bit better.

The ABC calculations are done in IBP where the results are getting persisted as attributes on product/location level. This attribute now should now be synchronized back to SAP ECC / S/4HANA, e.g. into field MARC~MAABC. I am convinced that the update should rather be performed by a released BAPI supposed to update the respective master data record instead of a direct table update.

So now it would be interesting to see a data flow example in CI-DS where e.g. IBP: MDT PRODUCT -> transformation/transfer setup -> S/4HANA: MARC
And: Is it mandatory to have the IBP addon installed on ERP side or is it possible to access BAPIs via an installed agent only (if that's is possible at all)?

BR, Peter

entry.title: Pioneering Datasphere: A Consultant’s Voyage Through SAP’s Data Evolution
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving world of data management and analytics, the journey to expertise is as dynamic as the technology itself. My adventure into the realm of SAP Datasphere serves as a testament to this continuous evolution. Starting out as an early adopter and consultant in this niche, I encountered a landscape where resources were scarce, and learning paths were as intricate as the data we sought to untangle. I remember vividly logging on for the first time over 4 years ago and figuring out what exactly I was looking at 😁.

The challenge was not just in mastering the technology, but in piecing together fragmented knowledge to build a cohesive understanding of a powerful tool that was set to redefine data warehousing in the cloud era.

Coming from an SAP BW world, this had its challenges, but it’s safe to say that SAP Datasphere can be an incredible tool for faster “time to information”. Or in simpler terms, a tool that shortens development cycles. That is, if you know what you’re doing 😄. This blog is here to help you out and give you all you need to scale your knowledge in a cloud world!

So, what again is SAP Datasphere?

SAP Datasphere, previously known as SAP Data Warehouse Cloud, is a cutting-edge cloud-based data management and analytics service. It is a unified service for data integration, cataloging, semantic modeling, data warehousing, and virtualizing workloads across all your data. SAP Datasphere facilitates data democratization, allowing users from both business and IT backgrounds to access, understand, and utilize their data effectively. With its persona-driven approach and powerful in-memory database SAP HANA Cloud as its foundation, SAP Datasphere enables real-time insights and decision-making across the enterprise, and is the technology foundation that supports a business data fabric architecture.

And things are looking bright for the future. New functionality is continuously added. At the event on March 6, 2024, SAP announced the following new capabilities:

  • Seamless planning (real time integration between SAP Analytics Cloud Planning and SAP Datasphere)
  • Generative AI with Joule
  • SAP Datasphere knowledge graph

The latter enables complex multi-hop queries by representing relationships between nodes as simple edges, keeping them easily traceable by LLMs. It will allow users to augment business context and evolve the graph structure in the ontology editor and use existing entities to pre-populate data for workflows in SAP Datasphere and Joule, saving time and effort for customers.

 Check out the executive blog!

The Dawn of SAP Datasphere

As I described, my initiation into SAP Datasphere, then a new technology, was met with enthusiasm and a fair share of trepidation. The promise of a unified, cloud-based data management platform was compelling, yet the path to proficiency was riddled with trial and error, piecemeal tutorials, and a community still finding its feet. The early days were characterized by a pioneering spirit, where each new discovery about SAP Datasphere‘s capabilities felt like a breakthrough. Great times, but how they have changed!

The Transformation: Access and Resources

Fast forward to today, and the landscape for learning SAP Datasphere has transformed dramatically. Almost as much as the transition from SAP Data Warehouse Cloud into SAP Datasphere.

What was once a niche, almost cryptic field, has now blossomed into a well-supported ecosystem brimming with resources, courses, and a vibrant community. This metamorphosis is not just about the availability of learning materials but represents a paradigm shift in how SAP nurtures its technologies and supports its practitioners.

 A World of Resources at Your Fingertips

The contrast between then and now could not be starker. SAP Learning now offers an expansive suite of resources tailored for SAP Datasphere enthusiasts of all levels. From the foundational courses that introduce the basics of SAP Datasphere set up and spaces, to advanced tutorials that delve into the nuances of data modeling and integration options.

Embracing the Community and Collaborative Learning

The SAP Community has evolved to become the backbone of the learning experience. Engaging with fellow SAP Datasphere consultants and enthusiasts offers a rich tapestry of insights, tips, and shared challenges that accelerate the learning process. The community's forums, blogs, and discussion groups serve as a real-time classroom where knowledge is both consumed and contributed, creating a dynamic learning environment that grows with the technology.

Strategic Partnerships and Expanding Capabilities

SAP's strategic partnerships have significantly expanded SAP Datasphere’s capabilities and, by extension, the learning opportunities for consultants. Collaborations with industry leaders in data management and AI, such as Collibra, Confluent, Databricks, and DataRobot, have enriched SAP Datasphere’s ecosystem, offering new dimensions of functionality and integration.

These partnerships not only enhance the platform's utility but also broaden the scope of skills and knowledge required to leverage SAP Datasphere fully, making you an even more valuable consultant.

The SAP Datasphere Guided experience: A Hands-on Approach to Learning

Perhaps the most transformative change has been the accessibility of SAP Datasphere through trial accounts. This hands-on experience allows consultants and newcomers alike to dive into the platform, experiment with its features, and apply what they've learned in a practical, risk-free environment. This direct engagement with SAP Datasphere is invaluable for cementing theoretical knowledge through practical application.

Looking Ahead: The Future of SAP Datasphere learning

As I reflect on my journey from the early days of limited resources to the present wealth of learning materials, community support, and strategic partnerships, it's clear that the path to becoming an SAP Datasphere consultant is more accessible and rewarding than ever before.

The evolution of the learning ecosystem around SAP Datasphere is a mirror to the platform's growth and sophistication. For those embarking on this journey, the message is clear: the resources, community, and opportunities are there, ripe for the taking. Now is the time to dive in, explore, and carve your niche in the future of cloud-based data management and analytics. The great part, remember SAP BW? SAP Datasphere has you covered with SAP Datasphere, SAP BW Bridge. Even your SAP BW skills will flourish even more. Who would have thought.

Embrace the journey, for in the world of SAP Datasphere, the possibilities are as boundless as the data itself!

And there is even more, introducing the SAP Certified Associate – Data Analyst - SAP Analytics Cloud!

It is the first role-based certification in the analytics area. It’s a comprehensive certification that covers the entire spectrum of a data analyst's role within SAP Analytics Cloud. From planning and to story design, predictive analytics, and administration – this certification is designed to empower you with the skills needed to navigate the intricate landscape of SAP Analytics Cloud.  

This is about mastering a full spectrum of tools and techniques to make you a well-rounded SAP Analytics Cloud expert – not only with theoretical aspects, but also with practical insights that can be applied in real-world scenarios. SAP has 13 learning journeys to help you prepare.

Get started by learning the basics with these three free Learning Journeys:

  • Exploring SAP Analytics Cloud | SAP Learning 
  • Designing Stories in SAP Analytics Cloud 
  • Leveraging SAP Analytics Cloud Functionality for Enterprise Planning | SAP Learning 

For those looking to dive deeper into SAP Datasphere, here's a curated list of essential online resources to kickstart or advance your journey:

  • SAP Learning Hub: Start with SAP's official learning platform, offering a range of courses from beginner to advanced levels tailored to SAP Datasphere.
  • openSAP Microlearning: For quick, bite-sized learning sessions on specific aspects of SAP Datasphere and related technologies.
  • SAP Community: A vibrant community where you can engage with other SAP Datasphere professionals, ask questions, and share insights.
  • SAP Developers: Tutorials and missions designed to provide hands-on experience with SAP Datasphere: 
  • SAP Training and Certification: Discover official training and certification programs to validate your SAP Datasphere expertise.
  • SAP Help Portal: The go-to place for comprehensive documentation on SAP Datasphere, including technical guides and product details.
  • Guided Experience | SAP Datasphere: Walk through key workflows and core features in a guided manner, receive direction on what to do and where to click, and get explanations and descriptions of actions and features.

entry.title: Re: Unable Transfer Deletion Flag from S4 to GTS
entry.content:
Hello Aditya, there is no deletion flag for products in GTS 11, as far as I am aware. GTS works on the concept of validity dates, so a material deleted today in S4 would show as validity end date = today in GTS. But I don't think this is visible to users, its at table level that the validity is stored. 

In our GTS custom coding, for various customs and compliance reasons, we do a RFC call back to ERP to check whether  the material is deleted in ERP. 

 

Best regards

Philip

entry.title: Can GTS transfer future classification to ERP at a future date?
entry.content:
Hello, as we know, GTS 11 allows classification into the future, but ERP has only the current commodity code assignment, not date dependent. I understand that in S4 HANA ERP the classification will also offer the ability to classify by date, but for this to work, both GTS S4  and S4 HANA must be implemented. This I feel is still a few years away. 

At our company users classify into the future, especially around year end before the new codes are active. It's a long task and takes time. Users have to classify and then must rememeber to trigger a transfer from GTS to ERP at the appropriate date.  

As an example: GTS has product 12345 classified now as A but next year will be B. So the user can do this in GTS, product 12345 is classified A until 31/03/2024 and is classified B from 01/04/2024 until 31/12/9999. Does the standard solution offer the possibility to transfer to ERP classification which is made for the future? So at the first day of validity (in this case 01/04/2024) of the new classification in GTS, this is automagically transferred to ERP and is ready for use without any intervention from user other than the initial task of classification?

Thanks

Regards

Philip

entry.title: Re: "CALL TRANSACTION 'VL33N' AND SKIP FIRST SCREEN" sometimes doesn't work, sometimes it
entry.content: Probably there is an intermediate screen that you don't see because it's closed due to SKIP FIRST SCREEN (= it's like pressing Enter).

entry.title: Experience our Demos at the SAP for Energy & Utilities Conference in Seville
entry.content:
Dear Utilities and OG&E Community,

Once again, the time has come! This year, in sunny and vibrant Seville; Seville has a special color says the song! From the 23rd to 25th of April, one of the most exciting industry events will take place yet again: SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference.

Our exhibition will feature a lineup of talks from esteemed customers, valued partners, and inspiring thought leaders. We aim to provide attendees with ample opportunities for inspiration and valuable insights into the hottest topics shaping our industry today.

Building on the overwhelming interest we've seen in our demo booth over the past two years, we're delighted to offer a wide array of solution demos once again. SAP Industry Experts will be on hand to showcase these solutions, answer your questions, provide personalized advice, and offer deep insights into the innovative solutions we bring to the table.

Demo Booth at SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference in Basel 2023

Curious about what awaits you at our booth? Here's a sneak peek into our lineup of solution demos of the most relevant SAP and Partner Solutions for the Utilities and Oil, Gas & Energy Industries you'll have the chance to explore at the event:

Utilities Demos:

Asset Management & Supply Chain

  • Smart Power Plant Demo: Experience live the insights of the SAP Smart Power Plant from the SAP Walldorf campus.
  • Geo Framework with SAP Enterprise Product Development Visualization of a Transformer Station: Users can leverage map-driven technology to streamline decision-making by seamlessly integrating business assets, such as equipment and maintenance orders, with geographic information systems (GIS). Through the Geographical Enablement Framework, companies can enhance efficiency and connectivity by enriching SAP Business Suite and SAP S/4HANA solutions with spatial awareness features, bridging the gap between GIS and business applications. This integration facilitates smoother workflows, empowering users to drive business processes directly from the map interface, thus achieving true data federation and optimizing decision-making capabilities.
  • Intelligent Asset Management e2e demo: a demo that walks through end to end process for Intelligent Asset Management solutions (SAP Enterprise Portfolio and Project Management, SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration, SAP Asset Performance Management, SAP S/4HANA Asset Management for Resource Scheduling, SAP Field Service Management, SAP Service and Asset Manager).

Customer Experience

  • SAP Service Cloud Version 2: Numerous trends are pushing utilities companies to adopt more customer-centric approaches and provide seamless and personalized service. Experience the SAP Service Cloud Utilities add-on to learn how you can orchestrate utilities processes directly from SAP Service Cloud​. Watch the video.
  • Digital Customer Self-Service and Engagement (SEW Self-Service): A customer engagement web and mobile solution that provides self service capabilities to utility customers and enables real-time, two-way communication between the utility and its customers. To stay competitive, utilities can no longer merely provide an online presence with basic account functions and a call center. Customers today demand digital service. Whether it’s web, mobile, or multichannel, customers would rather click, tap, and type than talk. SEW's solution will support a better interaction experience. 
  • SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement – Marketing Automation for Utilities: Accelerate time to value, meet customers where they are with what they want, and produce measurable results with real-time, scalable personalization.
  • Customer Service processes in the Customer Engagement: The interaction center agents can use the interaction center as a central point of entry to search and manage the Utilities master data as well as to carry out the traditional Utilities industry processes such as meter reading, bill correction, move-in/out and more. This demo is a walkthrough of features and functions available with this solution to help Utilities CSR to perform his / her job effectively.
  • Management of customer data and consent management with SAP Customer Data Platform

 Energy & Markets:

  • SAP Cloud for Energy: Get to know SAP Cloud for Energy, the platform for the complete metering data lifecycle management. Check out these videos Operational Monitoring, Solve Issues, Device Master Data, Configuration.
  • Running EnergyCommunity by Exnaton: The EnergyCommunity for SAP for Utilities is an innovative platform designed to help utilities companies calculate the energy exchange between prosumers using real-time data for more accurate billing and invoicing. Watch the video and try it for free
  • Managing the increasing complexity of metered installations in a world of Distributed Energy Resources with the Measurement Concept Management: SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation, also known as Measurement Concept Management, or MCM for short, is a new SAP solution aimed mainly at the distributed generation market. The solution serves to enable customers to manage, streamline and unify processes around installations including decentralized energy resources throughout their whole lifecycle, from their creation to the final shutdown. Read more about SAP Cloud for Utilities foundation here.

SAP S/4HANA Utilities / SAP Utilities Core

  • SAP Utilities Core: Meter to Cash and New Connection Process: SAP Utilities Core is our  solution offering addressing the core Utilities industry meter-to-cash process including customer service and market process management. Read more here 
  • Machine Learning for implausible meter readings and outsorted billing documents: The demo showcases the specific capabilities of machine learning-based processing for implausible meter readings. It is seamlessly integrated in meter reading processing in IS-U, learns from the meter reading and consumption history, and provides the capability to determine a machine learning release confidence used to automate the release of implausible meter readings.
  • Automatic Implausible MR Correction using Business Rules (BRFplus): One of the key processes for a Utility is the meter to cash process, which starts from the reading of the meter, billing and invoicing, and collecting the payment. At each step of the process, there are validation rules put in place to ensure the correct data is captured, billed and collected from the customer. The Demo focuses on the first step of the process capturing discrete meter reads from external systems and performing validations. This function is part of the S4 Utilities solution and is executed by Device Management.
  • SAP FIORI Apps Billing and Meter Reading: See the new user experience for dedicated metering and billing roles and the possibilities to branch from KPI-bar charts through list views into a detailed single customer case.
  • Automatizing Processes for Utilities - SAP MPM: Reoccurring processes are a common phenomenon across utilities companies. Automizing them serves customers with real business value. Market process management for utilities is a solution that enables you to efficiently model, execute, and monitor your business processes, including message handling for communication with other systems (such as market partners).
  • SAP Video Data Story … Utilities go Gen Z: Many Utility customers complain about their bill being way too complex. Short-form video platforms became the de-facto information sharing standard, not only for GenZ. Video Data Stories empowers you to translate bill's complexity into a snappy, animated, and convenient format to be understood within seconds.
  • Business Process Exception Management - KPI Designer: The focus of this Demo will be utilizing the Business Process Exception Management (BPEM) data to illustrate how the application can improve the management of exceptions. Charts and tables will be created to view data. Semantic objects will be defined to provide the ability to execute business processes and/or transactions against the BPEM data without logging into another application.

Water

  • Intelligent Water Management Platform: Transform your company into a Data Driven Organization by implementing new and innovative business scenarios. The following demos contain information and use cases:
    • Smart Water Platform  for Remote Water Quality Monitoring and Leakage Detection: Get insights on this platform watching this video
    • Smart Water Platform use cases for  Water leakage and remote water quality analysis: Watch the video to get more insights
  • SAP Utilities Core for Water: Meter to cash for Water platform in SAP S/4HANA: Reduce architecture complexity and gain operational efficiency moving into a single. Get some insights watching this video

Utilities Analytics:

  • SAP Analytics Cloud: Dashboard for Utilities: Gain insights into how advanced analytics and predictive processes can help utilities companies use leading-edge tools to reduce the impact of service interruptions and improve customer experience. Watch the video
  • Analytics, Billing and Meter Reading: Learn how to easily configure your own individualized report based standard analytical scenarios in SAP S/4HANA Utilities.
  • Enterprise Maturity Assessment for Utilities: Assess your business capabilities, identify areas for improvement, and gain valuable strategies for success. By just spending around 20 minutes you will receive a personalized report that compares your company scores with industry benchmarks, highlights priority opportunities, and provides best practice content.

 

Oil, Gas & Energy Demos:

Shutdown Turnaround & Outage Solution for Refineries by Wipro: This solution jointly developed with WIPRO as our partner where you will see comprehensive and integrated STO management solution. Which addresses customer challenges as diverse as end-to-end STO processes, scope management, non-integrated planning, long lead items tracking, safety, scheduling, real-time effective reporting, control of costs, risks, procurement and user friendliness.

SAP Analytics Cloud: Dashboard for Oil, Gas & Energy: Customers can view Digital Boardroom to transform their executive meetings. Replace static presentations and stale information with interactive discussions based on real data – allowing you to make fact-based decisions to drive your business. These dashboards fulfil the reporting requirements at all levels.

SAP Field Logistics Management - Efficiently manage Upstream remote logistics: In this demo Customers can see end to end process of how SAP field logistics solution helps to manage the movement of products required for maintenance operations between a supplying base and a remote location.

Hydrocarbon Supply chain management using OGE solution: Follow the molecule of  oil and gas products from trade to supply, logistics and gain insights on finance and operations.

 

Cross-Industry Demos:

E-Mobility - Billing & operations of EV charging services: 

  • EV charging point management with SAP E-Mobility.
  • EV fleet charging management and EV charging solutions for condominiums
  • Efficient and integrated billing of EV charging cards and charging solution offerings with SAP Subscription Billing and S/4HANA.
  • Billing & operations of EV charging services with SAP: Build, run, and manage electric vehicle charging networks with integration into end-to-end billing processes on one platform. Get more details.

Sustainability – Carbon Tracking and Reporting: Here Customers can experience how they can track, record, report, and act with SAP Sustainability solutions and achieve Net Zero.

Predictive Maintenance using the SAP BTP: Customer can see the power of SAP BTP and how SAP BTP and SAP Analytics Cloud is used to build predictive models for abnormal events based on sensor data and to provide the results through user-friendly dashboards and e-mail notifications.

Digital Twins driving the future of Business: Digital twins are a virtual representation of a physical object. It could be anything – as complex as a car or a manufacturing production line, or as simple as a piece of furniture. The digital twin emulates all the parts of the object (or set of connected objects) to create a virtual proxy. A car's digital twin would model its shape, tires, seats, engine, transmission, everything. Companies use a digital twin to design a 3D model of the original, enabling teams to analyze the performance of the object under different conditions. Successfully deployed, digital twins can save serious money, improve product designs, and elevate efficiency and productivity.

SAP Subscription Billing - how to easy create non commodity charges: Join us in an open discussion to understand the key trends and new non-commodity models that companies are operating and how we can model a simple use case (real time demo) in SAP Subscription Billing. After this session you should be able to understand the capabilities and the operations that you can use within SAP Subscription Billing.

SAP Convergent Invoicing - aggregation of commodity and non-commodity: Do you have side business that are running outside of SAP system? Do you want to include new business models within you SAP system? Choose your pace to start using a single source of true for your billing and invoicing generation by leveraging the key capabilities of SAP Convergent Invoicing and how it can be integrated with your SAP S4/HANA Utilities system and others. The session will give you an overview of the BRIM portfolio (in Private Cloud) and the key capabilities of SAP Convergent Invoicing. At the end of this session you should have the confidence on how the solution can help your business to scale and perform consolidated information (amongst others).

SAP CPQ (Configure, Price, and Quote) for B2C and B2B Offers and Quotations

Business AI for Energy and Utilities

SAP Signavio:

    • SAP S/4HANA Transformation with SAP Signavio for Utilities
    • SAP Signavio Sustainability for the Energy Sector, get information on our website
    • Business process improvements for Oil, Gas and Energy using Signavio: Customers will experience how they can leverage SAP Signavio to analyze and look into your business processes to remodel and automate them in order to deliver a highly optimized and automated business culture.

 

Take this unique opportunity to connect with you peers, get insights into industry trends and chart your development plan with the latest technologies of 2024!

 

See you in Seville!
